lines: Past the low wall, in the cold, they have hammered up the next one. The nails are fresh. The black-gold has only begun to climb its arms, and the halo is still mostly bone-bud, soft and wet.
A Choir tally is pinned to the post beside it: 'First on the altar. This one for after. Do not start it early -- hunger ruins the lesson.'
It hears you breathing and tries, very slowly, to shape a word it has heard the other one whisper upstairs. Whatever you choose down here is yours to carry up with you.

effects.log: You leave it bound in the dark. It is still trying to find the word as you climb away.
You end it before the Choir can. The cold gets a little colder, and a little quieter.

lines: It sags on the nails and stops trying to speak. One fewer saint for the road to Hallowfen.
Whether that was mercy or meddling, you will not know until you reach the place this chapel keeps pointing at.
You leave it hanging, half-made, waiting its turn behind the low wall.
The Choir keeps a patient larder. When the first saint is gone, this one will already be ready, and someone will have to come back down here.
